 {"id":"bd-3y1n","title":"EPIC: No-Brainer ACFS Improvements (Jan 2026)","description":"## Epic: No-Brainer ACFS Improvements (Jan 2026)

This epic contains 15 high-impact, low-complexity improvements to the ACFS project that will obviously benefit users with minimal risk. Each improvement was evaluated against these criteria:

**Evaluation Criteria:**
- Robust: Does it make the system more resilient?
- Reliable: Does it reduce failure modes?
- Performant: Does it make things faster?
- Intuitive: Is it discoverable and obvious?
- User-friendly: Does it reduce friction?
- Ergonomic: Does it fit natural workflows?
- Useful: Does it solve real problems?
- Compelling: Will users be glad it exists?
- Accretive: Does it build on existing strengths?
- Pragmatic: Is the effort justified by the benefit?

## Top 5 (Highest Impact)
1. **bd-3nbx**: `acfs doctor --fix` - Auto-remediation mode
2. **bd-2dkb**: Copy-to-clipboard for web wizard code blocks
3. **bd-29fl**: `acfs status` - One-line health summary
4. **bd-zhdi**: Shell tab completion for acfs command
5. **bd-2gys**: Step validation in web wizard

## Next 10 (High Value, Complementary)
6. **bd-21kh**: Progress bar during tool installation
7. **bd-39ye**: NO_COLOR environment variable support
8. **bd-1lug**: Rate limit backoff for GitHub API calls
9. **bd-1eop**: Skip already-installed tools during install
10. **bd-w8fx**: VPS provider comparison table
11. **bd-1yfv**: "I'm stuck" help button in web wizard
12. **bd-2p56**: `acfs changelog` command
13. **bd-331g**: Dark mode toggle in web wizard
14. **bd-2zqr**: Webhook notification on install completion
15. **bd-3jd9**: `acfs export-config` command

## Implementation Notes
- Each bead is self-contained with full implementation details
- Start with top 5 for maximum early impact
- Beads 6-10 are quick wins that complement the top 5
- Beads 11-15 are nice-to-haves that round out the experience

## Success Criteria
- All 15 beads implemented and tested
- No regressions in existing functionality
- Documentation updated for new features

 {"id":"bd-47sq","title":"Deep exploration: MS (Meta Skill)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of MS (Meta Skill) and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
# Verify MS installation
[[ -d /dp/meta_skill ]] && echo "PASS: ms repo exists" || { echo "FAIL: ms repo missing"; exit 1; }
command -v ms &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: ms command available" || { echo "FAIL: ms not in PATH"; exit 1; }

# Check Claude Code skill integration
[[ -d ~/.claude/skills ]] && echo "INFO: Claude skills directory exists" || echo "INFO: No skills directory yet"
```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State)
```bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/ms-exploration-snapshots-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
echo "Snapshots saved to $SNAPSHOT_DIR"
```

### 0.3 TypeScript Interface Reference
Content must match FlywheelTool interface:
- id, name, tagline, description, deepDescription
- features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[]
- integrationLevel, category, status

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
- `cat /dp/meta_skill/README.md` - Read full README
- Check for skill authoring docs, SKILL.md format

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Launch code investigation agent to understand:
  - Skill creation workflow
  - SKILL.md format and requirements
  - Skill validation mechanism
  - Installation into Claude Code
  - Skill discovery and loading

### 1.3 CLI Command Verification
```bash
# Verify each documented command works
ms --help 2>&1 | head -20
ms create --help 2>&1 | head -10
ms validate --help 2>&1 | head -10

# Test actual functionality
ms list 2>&1 | head -10
```

### 1.4 External Context Search
- `/xf search 'meta skill OR ms OR skill authoring'` - Twitter archive
- `cass search 'ms skill create' --robot --limit 10` - Past sessions

### 1.5 Project State Review
- Check beads in /dp/meta_skill/.beads/
- Review recent commits: `cd /dp/meta_skill && git log --oneline -20`

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING)

### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification
Document findings for each area:
- [ ] Skill creation workflow
- [ ] SKILL.md format specification
- [ ] Validation rules and errors
- [ ] Installation process
- [ ] Skill discovery mechanism

### 2.2 Synergy Verification
Cross-reference these tools actually integrate:
- [ ] Claude Code - primary integration target
- [ ] sc (skill) - skill creation helper
- [ ] sw (skill writer) - advanced skill authoring

### 2.3 Format Verification
```bash
# Check SKILL.md template
cat /dp/meta_skill/templates/SKILL.md 2>/dev/null | head -30
# Or find example skills
find /dp -name "SKILL.md" 2>/dev/null | head -5 | xargs head -20
```

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS)

### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
Update ms entry with VERIFIED information:
- `tagline`: Skill authoring for Claude Code
- `description`: Creation and validation
- `deepDescription`: How skill system works
- `features`: Verified capabilities
- `cliCommands`: Only commands that actually work
- `connectsTo`: Only verified integrations

### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
Update TldrFlywheelTool entry with:
- `briefDescription`: Technical summary
- `bulletPoints`: Verified capabilities
- `synergyExamples`: Working integration examples

## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY CHANGES)

### 4.1 TypeScript Compilation
```bash
cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20
```

### 4.2 Unit Tests
```bash
# Test ms entry structure
node -e "
const { flywheelTools } = require('./lib/flywheel');
const ms = flywheelTools.find(t => t.id === 'ms');
console.log('Testing ms entry...');
console.assert(ms, 'ms entry exists');
console.assert(ms.features?.length > 0, 'has features');
console.assert(ms.cliCommands?.length > 0, 'has commands');
console.log('All assertions passed');
"
```

### 4.3 E2E Test: Skill Operations
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG=/tmp/ms-e2e-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log

echo "=== MS E2E Test ===" | tee $LOG
echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a $LOG

# Test 1: List skills
echo "Test 1: List skills..." | tee -a $LOG
ms list 2>&1 | head -10 | tee -a $LOG

# Test 2: Validate a skill
echo "Test 2: Validate..." | tee -a $LOG
ms validate /dp/meta_skill 2>&1 | head -10 | tee -a $LOG || echo "Validation done"

# Test 3: Create help
echo "Test 3: Create help..." | tee -a $LOG
ms create --help 2>&1 | head -5 | tee -a $LOG

echo "=== All Tests Passed ===" | tee -a $LOG
echo "Log: $LOG"
```

### 4.4 Content Diff Verification
```bash
echo "=== Changes Made ==="
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts || true
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts || true
```

## Phase 5: Completion (FINALIZE)

### 5.1 Rollback Procedure (if tests fail)
```bash
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
echo "Rolled back to pre-exploration state"
```

### 5.2 Sync Changes
```bash
br update bd-47sq --status done
br sync --flush-only
```

### 5.3 Final Verification
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors
- [ ] Skill format documented correctly
- [ ] No broken links or references","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:01:36.485651577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T03:32:05.943130894Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T03:32:05.943105255Z","close_reason":"MS deep exploration complete: verified v0.1.0, 60+ commands, 12 MCP tools (corrected from 6). BACKGROUND:

WHAT IT DOES:

- Triggers local builds using nektos/act (reuses exact GH Actions workflow YAML)
- Distributes builds across platforms: Linux (Docker+act), macOS (SSH to mmini), Windows (SSH to wlap)
- Supply chain security: minisign signatures, SBOM generation (syft), SLSA provenance
- Uploads releases to GitHub with checksums and signatures
- Generates smart curl-bash installers with staleness detection
- Has repo configs for 20+ Dicklesworthstone projects (ntm, bv, cass, etc.)

WHY IT MATTERS FOR FLYWHEEL:
- Ensures tool releases aren't blocked by GitHub Actions throttling
- Critical for CI/CD resilience when releasing frequently
- Cross-platform build capability supports diverse target environments
- Supply chain security features (signing, SBOM, SLSA) important for trust

 {"id":"bd-53zvp","title":"Test: verify claude update --channel latest works correctly","description":"## What
Verify that the --channel latest fix actually works end-to-end.

## Test Plan

### Pre-flight check
1. Run `claude --version` to record current version and channel
2. Run `claude update --help` to confirm --channel flag exists

### Test 1: Dry-run validation of update.sh
1. Run `bash scripts/lib/update.sh --dry-run --verbose 2>&1 | grep -i "claude update"` 
2. Verify: ALL logged claude update commands include "--channel latest"
3. Verify: No bare "claude update" appears in the dry-run output
4. Log: All claude update commands found in dry-run output

### Test 2: uca alias validation
1. Source acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc
2. Run `alias uca` or `type uca`
3. Verify: alias contains "--channel latest"
4. Log: Full alias definition

### Test 3: Live update test (if safe)
1. Run `claude update --channel latest 2>&1`
2. Verify: exit code 0
3. Verify: version output shows "latest" channel or version >= current
4. Log: Full update output, before/after versions

### Test 4: Grep-based completeness check
1. Run `grep -n "claude update" scripts/lib/update.sh | grep -v "channel"` 
2. Verify: NO matches (every occurrence has --channel)
3. Also check: `grep -n "claude update" acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc | grep -v "channel"`
4. Verify: NO matches
5. Log: Any remaining bare "claude update" occurrences

## Output Format
```
[TEST_1] PASS: All dry-run claude update commands include --channel latest (5/5)
[TEST_2] PASS: uca alias = "~/.local/bin/claude update --channel latest"
[TEST_3] PASS: Live update succeeded, channel=latest version=2.1.37
[TEST_4] PASS: No bare "claude update" found in update.sh or acfs.zshrc
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 4 tests pass
- Zero bare "claude update" anywhere in the codebase","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:35:47.930709260Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:41:41.035926441Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:41:41.035906184Z","close_reason":"Superseded by bd-gsjqf.4.  {"id":"bd-5mes9","title":"Update all dependencies to latest stable versions","description":"## Dependency Update for agentic_coding_flywheel_setup

### Context
ACFS is primarily Bash scripts + a Bun/TypeScript manifest generator. Dependencies are:
- Bun packages in `packages/manifest/package.json`
- System tools installed by the setup scripts
- Shell libraries in `scripts/lib/`

### Steps
1. `cd /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup`
2. Update Bun packages: `cd packages/manifest && bun update && cd ../..`
3. Regenerate manifest index: `cd packages/manifest && bun run generate && cd ../..`
4. Verify manifest SHA256 matches: `scripts/check-manifest-drift.sh --json`
5. Test installer: run a dry-run or check syntax with `bash -n install.sh`
6. Verify acfs CLI works: `source acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc && acfs doctor`

### Key Dependencies to Watch
- `@types/node` -- TypeScript definitions
- `yaml` -- YAML parsing in manifest generator
- `zod` -- schema validation (if used)
- System tools: claude, codex, gemini CLI tools

### Verification
- Manifest regeneration produces valid output
- `install.sh` syntax check passes
- `acfs doctor` passes all checks
- SHA256 hash in manifest_index.sh matches acfs.manifest.yaml","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:19:53.136401139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T17:11:21.158286117Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T17:11:21.158262793Z","close_reason":"Updated Bun packages to latest stable versions, regenerated manifest, verified SHA256 integrity","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"bd-alqyx","title":"EPIC: Integrate storage_ballast_helper (sbh) into ACFS","description":"Integrate storage_ballast_helper — a production-grade Rust daemon preventing disk-full scenarios via ballast files, artifact scanning, and special location monitoring.

BACKGROUND:
- Repo: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/storage_ballast_helper
- Binary: sbh
- Tech: Rust (edition 2024), single crate, #![forbid(unsafe_code)], NO async runtime (uses OS threads + crossbeam)
- Install: curl -fsSL .../scripts/install.sh | bash
- Version: 0.3.15
- License: MIT

WHAT IT DOES:
- Three-pronged defense: ballast files (pre-allocated sacrificial space), artifact scanner (safe stale build cleanup), special location monitor (/tmp, /dev/shm)
- EWMA + PID controller for predictive pressure control
- Multi-factor artifact scoring with hard vetoes (.git protected, open file handles, too-recent files)
- Progressive rollout: shadow → canary → enforce modes
- Explainable decisions with evidence ledger
- Zero-write emergency mode for near-100% full disk recovery
- Project protection via .sbh-protect markers
- systemd service integration

WHY IT MATTERS FOR FLYWHEEL:
- Agent swarms generate gigabytes of build artifacts per hour
- Disk-full is catastrophic: builds fail, SQLite corrupts, daemon state lost
- sbh daemon continuously monitors and reacts before problems occur
- Designed specifically for the multi-agent VPS use case

+{"id":"bd-c83ac","title":"EPIC: Integrate post_compact_reminder (pcr) into ACFS","description":"Integrate post_compact_reminder — a Claude Code hook that reminds Claude to re-read AGENTS.md after context compaction.

BACKGROUND:
- Repo: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/post_compact_reminder
- Script: claude-post-compact-reminder (installed to ~/.local/bin/)
- Installer: install-post-compact-reminder.sh (2749 lines)
- Tech: Pure Bash 4.0+, requires jq, python3
- Version: 1.2.5
- License: MIT + OpenAI/Anthropic Rider

WHAT IT DOES:
- Hooks into Claude Code's SessionStart event with matcher: "compact"
- After context compaction, outputs a mandatory reminder to re-read AGENTS.md
- Four built-in templates: minimal, detailed, checklist, default
- All templates require confirmation before proceeding
- Atomic settings.json modifications with backups
- Self-updating capability
- Comprehensive health checks (--status, --doctor)

WHY IT MATTERS FOR FLYWHEEL:
- Context compaction is the #1 cause of Claude "going rogue" — forgetting project rules
- After compaction, Claude loses all memory of AGENTS.md conventions
- This hook forces Claude to re-read rules before continuing work

- Upstream: Dicklesworthstone/meta_skill release CI matrix

 {"id":"bd-gsjqf","title":"Fix update.sh Claude Code channel: bare 'claude update' downgrades to stable (GH #125)","description":"## Context
GitHub issue #125 filed by joyshmitz (2026-02-08). The install path was fixed (commit 4d0174f0 — agents.sh now uses @latest), but update.sh still calls bare "claude update" which forces the stable channel.

## Problem
run_cmd_claude_update() in scripts/lib/update.sh (line 1230) calls bare "claude update" at lines 1254, 1260, 1267, 1269, 1274. This always uses the stable channel. On a SUCCESSFUL update, users get silently downgraded from latest (e.g., 2.1.37) to stable (e.g., 2.1.25). The fallback path (update_run_verified_installer claude latest at line 1157) correctly uses latest, but only fires when "claude update" FAILS.

Additionally, the uca alias in acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc line 158 uses bare "~/.local/bin/claude update" (also stable channel). The full alias chains claude update with codex and gemini updates: alias uca='~/.local/bin/claude update && (bun install -g --trust @openai/codex@latest || bun install -g --trust @openai/codex) && bun install -g --trust @google/gemini-cli@latest'

## CRITICAL: --channel flag DOES NOT EXIST
Verified by running: claude update --help => only -h/--help available. The originally proposed fix (claude update --channel latest) is INVALID. This was confirmed by running the actual command on this VPS.

## Chosen Fix: Replace run_cmd_claude_update() with verified installer
The update_run_verified_installer function already exists at update.sh:715-737 and correctly handles the latest channel. It:
1. Looks up the installer URL from KNOWN_INSTALLERS[claude] = "https://claude.ai/install.sh" (security.sh:183)
2. Fetches the installer script
3. Verifies its SHA-256 checksum against checksums.yaml
4. Pipes to bash with args (e.g., "latest")
The fallback path at line 1157 already uses this: run_cmd "Claude Code (reinstall)" update_run_verified_installer claude latest

The fix replaces the entire run_cmd_claude_update() function body with a call to update_run_verified_installer, preserving the logging/error-handling wrapper while switching the underlying mechanism from bare "claude update" to the verified installer with the "latest" argument.

For the uca alias, replace the "~/.local/bin/claude update" portion with an inline verified-installer invocation, OR redirect through a wrapper script, OR call the update function. The alias must preserve the codex and gemini update chain.

## Affected Files
- scripts/lib/update.sh (lines 1229-1296, run_cmd_claude_update(); lines 1150-1161, caller)
- acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc (line 158, uca alias)
- scripts/lib/security.sh (line 183, KNOWN_INSTALLERS[claude] — reference only)
- scripts/lib/agents.sh (line 21, CLAUDE_PACKAGE — reference only; line 148, verified installer call — reference for pattern)

## Sub-tasks (see child beads)
1. Audit: scan for ALL bare "claude update" occurrences (bd-gsjqf.1)
2. Rewrite run_cmd_claude_update() to use verified installer (bd-gsjqf.2)
3. Fix uca alias and any other occurrences found in audit (bd-gsjqf.3)
4. Comprehensive testing (bd-gsjqf.4)
5. +{"id":"bd-y691c","title":"EPIC: Integrate cross_agent_session_resumer (casr) into ACFS","description":"Integrate cross_agent_session_resumer — a CLI tool that converts AI coding agent sessions across providers for seamless cross-provider resumption.

BACKGROUND:
- Repo: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/cross_agent_session_resumer
- Binary: casr
- Tech: Rust 2024 (nightly), uses clap, serde, rusqlite, tracing
- Install: curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash
- Version: active development
- License: MIT + OpenAI/Anthropic Rider

WHAT IT DOES:
- Reads sessions from any installed provider into a canonical intermediate representation (IR)
- Writes back to target provider's native format for seamless resumption
- Supports 14 providers: Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, Cursor, Cline, Aider, Amp, OpenCode, ChatGPT, and more

 {"id":"bd-zhdi","title":"Shell tab completion for acfs command","description":"## Overview
Add shell tab completion for the `acfs` command (bash and zsh).

## Current Problem
- Users must remember all subcommands
- No discovery mechanism for available options
- Typos in command names cause errors

## Proposed Completion
```bash
$ acfs <TAB>
doctor    install   newproj   status    update

$ acfs doctor --<TAB>
--fix     --json    --quiet   --verbose

$ acfs install <TAB>
all       core      dev       shell     # module names from manifest
```

## Implementation Details
1. Generate completions from manifest.yaml at install time
2. Support both bash and zsh
3. Install to standard locations:
   - Bash: ~/.local/share/bash-completion/completions/acfs
   - Zsh: ~/.local/share/zsh/site-functions/_acfs
4. Source automatically via shell rc files

## Completion Sources
- Static: command names (doctor, install, update, etc.)
- Dynamic: module names from manifest.yaml
- Dynamic: tool names for granular install

## Installation
```bash
# Added to install.sh
acfs_install_completions() {
  local shell="$1"
  case "$shell" in
    bash) install_bash_completions ;;
    zsh)  install_zsh_completions ;;
  esac
}
```

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test bash completion for all commands
- [ ] Test zsh completion for all commands
- [ ] Test dynamic module name completion
- [ ] Test completion after fresh install
- [ ] Test update preserves completions

## Files to Create
- scripts/completions/acfs.bash
- scripts/completions/_acfs (zsh)
- Modify: scripts/install.sh (install completions)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:01:32.204228108Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T02:05:11.176778479Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T02:05:11.176702335Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zhdi","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:03:36.006705246Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
